properties.name: Muggleton Road, Amesbury, Wiltshire
properties.summary: In June 2018, Dawn Sturgess, who had no connection to espionage, was fatally poisoned after coming into contact with a discarded Novichok-laced perfume bottle in Amesbury, Wiltshire.  Her boyfriend, C
properties.notes: harlie Rowley,  found a perfume bottle containing Novichok in a charity bin or on the street in Amesbury-it was believed to have been discarded by the same Russian GRU operatives who poisoned Sergei and Yulia Skripal.  Rowley gave the bottle to Sturgess as a gift, believing it was perfume.  She sprayed it directly onto her wrists and withing 15 minutes, fell severely ill.  She was rushed to Salisbury District Hospital, where she died after 8 days.  Rowley was also contaminated but survived after falling into a coma and undergoing treatment. 9. City Stay Hotel (Bow Road, London) -- The GRU operatives who carried out the Skripal attack stayed here before traveling to Salisbury. Novichok traces were later discovered in their room.

properties.name: Zizzi Restaurant
properties.summary: Part of a UK-wide Italian restaurant chain, the restaurant was one of the last places the Skripals visited before they were found unconscious.  The Skripals dined at Zizzi in the afternoon. They were
properties.notes: seen behaving normally while eating but began to show symptoms of poisoning soon after leaving the restaurant. Shortly after, they collapsed on a bench near The Maltings shopping area.Following the discovery that they had been poisoned with Novichok, Zizzi was immediately sealed off as a crime scene. Investigators found traces of the nerve agent at the restaurant, particularly on the tableware and chairs used by the Skripals. The contamination led to the restaurant's prolonged closure while it underwent deep cleaning and decontamination. The Maltings Bench (27 The Maltings)-   Shortly after 4:15 PM, passersby noticed them slumped on a bench in The Maltings shopping area.  Witnesses described Sergei Skripal as staring blankly, unresponsive, while Yulia was foaming at the mouth, appearing rigid and unconscious.  First responders initially treated the incident as a possible opioid overdose, given the Skripals' sudden collapse. Upon arrival at Salisbury District Hospital, doctors quickly realized their symptoms were not consistent with drug poisoning.   The UK government later confirmed they had been exposed to Novichok, a Soviet-era military-grade nerve agent.

properties.name: The Ship Tavern
properties.summary: Hidden gem near Lincoln's Inn Fields, dating back centuries. Popular with lawyers and locals.

properties.name: The George Inn, Norton St Philip
properties.summary: One of England's oldest inns, dating from 1397. The Duke of Monmouth used it as HQ during the 1685 rebellion. Samuel Pepys stayed here.

properties.name: Australia House (Gringotts)
properties.summary: The grand marble interior of the Australian High Commission was used as Gringotts Wizarding Bank. The ornate banking hall with its chandeliers is unmistakable.

properties.name: King's Cross Platform 9 3/4
properties.summary: The famous platform where Hogwarts students catch the Express. A photo opportunity with a trolley disappearing into the wall is set up in the station concourse.

properties.name: Millennium Bridge
properties.summary: The pedestrian bridge was spectacularly destroyed by Death Eaters in Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince. Connects St Paul's to Tate Modern.

properties.name: Reptile House, London Zoo
properties.summary: Where Harry first discovers he can talk to snakes in Philosopher's Stone. He accidentally sets a Burmese python free by making the glass vanish.

properties.name: St Pancras Renaissance Hotel
properties.summary: The stunning Gothic exterior was used for exterior shots of King's Cross Station. Ron and Harry fly the Ford Anglia past it in Chamber of Secrets.

properties.name: Lambeth Bridge
properties.summary: The Knight Bus squeezes between two red double-deckers on this bridge in Prisoner of Azkaban.

properties.name: Piccadilly Circus
properties.summary: Harry, Ron, and Hermione apparate into Piccadilly Circus in Deathly Hallows Part 1, nearly getting hit by a bus on Shaftesbury Avenue.

properties.name: MI6 Building (SIS)
properties.summary: The distinctive green and cream headquarters of the Secret Intelligence Service on the Thames. Featured in GoldenEye, The World Is Not Enough, and Skyfall (where it gets blown up).

properties.name: Treasury Building, Whitehall
properties.summary: The view from M's office in several Bond films looks out over Horse Guards Parade. The building itself doubles as MI6 interiors.

properties.name: Somerset House
properties.summary: Used in Tomorrow Never Dies and Goldeneye. The grand neoclassical courtyard with its fountains has also appeared in Sherlock Holmes and many other films.

properties.name: Tower Bridge
properties.summary: Bond drives a speedboat under the bridge in The World Is Not Enough and it appears in Die Another Day. One of London's most iconic landmarks.

properties.name: Barbican Centre
properties.summary: The brutalist arts complex doubled as Shanghai in Skyfall. Its distinctive architectural style has made it a popular filming location.

properties.name: Natural History Museum
properties.summary: Featured in Spectre where Bond chases Mr. Hinx. Also a Paddington filming location. One of London's finest free museums with dinosaur galleries.

properties.name: Speedy's Cafe
properties.summary: The cafe next door to 221B Baker Street in BBC's Sherlock. Located at 187 North Gower Street, it became a pilgrimage site for fans of the Benedict Cumberbatch series.

properties.name: St Bartholomew's Hospital
properties.summary: Where Sherlock fakes his death by jumping from the roof in 'The Reichenbach Fall' (BBC Sherlock). Also where Watson and Holmes first meet in Conan Doyle's original stories.

properties.name: The Criterion Restaurant
properties.summary: Where Dr Watson meets Stamford in A Study in Scarlet, leading to his introduction to Sherlock Holmes. The ornate Neo-Byzantine interior is stunning.

properties.name: Portland Place
properties.summary: Used as the exterior of 221B Baker Street in BBC's Sherlock. The grand Georgian terrace provided the perfect backdrop for the famous front door.

properties.name: Lancaster House
properties.summary: Doubles as Buckingham Palace interiors in The Crown. Its lavish state rooms with gilded ceilings are even grander than the real palace. Open for occasional public tours.

properties.name: Wilton House
properties.summary: The Double Cube Room doubles as Buckingham Palace in The Crown. Also used in Pride and Prejudice, Sense and Sensibility, and Emma. Magnificent Palladian architecture.

properties.name: Paddington Station
properties.summary: Where the Brown family discovers Paddington Bear sitting on his suitcase. Brunel's grand iron and glass trainshed. There's a bronze Paddington statue on Platform 1.

properties.name: Chalcot Crescent (Browns' House)
properties.summary: The pastel-coloured terraced street in Primrose Hill where the Brown family home is located in the Paddington films. Number 30 is the famous blue door.

properties.name: Tower of London
properties.summary: Featured in Paddington 2 during the thrilling chase scene. Nearly 1000 years of history as a royal palace, prison, and home of the Crown Jewels.

properties.name: St Paul's Cathedral
properties.summary: Features in Paddington 2 and countless other films. Wren's masterpiece survived the Blitz. Climb 528 steps to the Golden Gallery for panoramic views.

properties.name: Battersea Power Station
properties.summary: Featured in The Great Muppet Caper. Now a major redevelopment with shops, restaurants, and apartments. The iconic four chimneys are a London landmark.

properties.name: The Travel Bookshop (Notting Hill)
properties.summary: The inspiration for Hugh Grant's bookshop in Notting Hill (1999). The original shop at 13 Blenheim Crescent is now a different business but the blue door at 280 Westbourne Park Road remains famous.

properties.name: Kenwood House
properties.summary: The bench where Hugh Grant and Julia Roberts have their final scene in Notting Hill. A beautiful neoclassical villa on Hampstead Heath with free entry and a stunning art collection.

properties.name: Borough Market
properties.summary: Bridget Jones's flat is above the Globe pub in Borough Market. London's oldest food market (1014) with incredible street food, artisan produce, and a vibrant atmosphere.

properties.name: Royal Exchange
properties.summary: Where Mark Darcy works in Bridget Jones. The grand colonnaded building near Bank station now houses luxury shops and restaurants.

properties.name: Black Prince Pub
properties.summary: Doubles as the Black Prince pub in Kingsman: The Secret Service. The real pub is a local favourite in Kennington, near the Imperial War Museum.

properties.name: Huntsman Savile Row
properties.summary: The Kingsman tailor shop is inspired by the real Huntsman at 11 Savile Row. The famous street has been the home of bespoke tailoring since the 1730s.

properties.name: Tate Modern
properties.summary: Featured in Mission: Impossible - Fallout during a tense chase scene. The former Bankside Power Station is now one of the world's most visited modern art galleries. Free entry.
